items.title: [D] AMA Secure version of OpenClaw
items.summary: Open-source Rust runtime IronClaw: security-focused agent runtime for OpenClaw with DB-backed memory, WASM sandboxed tools, credential protection, prompt-injection mitigations, and audit capabilities. GitHub: github.com/nearai/ironclaw

items.title: [D] A mathematical proof from an anonymous Korean forum: The essence of Attention is fundamentally a d^2 problem, not n^2. (PDF included)
items.summary: Post linking an anonymous paper claiming Attention's intrinsic optimization geometry is d^2-dimensional (not n^2), proposing CSQ (Centered Shifted-Quadratic) Attention to reduce complexity and replace softmax; requests expert verification.

items.title: [D] Has anyone read Blaise Agüera y Arcas' What is Intelligence?
items.summary: Reddit discussion asking for impressions of Blaise Agüera y Arcas' 'What is Intelligence?'—early sections suggest big claims; poster requests community input.

items.title: [P] Bypassing CoreML to natively train a 110M Transformer on the Apple Neural Engine (Orion)
items.summary: ORION: open-source system enabling multi-step training of a 110M transformer on Apple ANE by bypassing CoreML, custom compiler pipeline, techniques to avoid repeated recompilation; repo: github.com/mechramc/Orion.

items.title: [D] Ijcai 2026 reviews
items.summary: Short thread asking whether people have received IJCAI 2026 reviews and expectations for outcomes; also asks for tips on the chairing tool.

items.title: [D] Impact of EU AI Act on your work?
items.summary: Researcher asks how the EU AI Act affects practitioners working on models classified as high-risk (e.g., credit scoring, insurance) and whether small-scale testing practices are constrained by compliance burdens.

items.title: [R] Are keywords necessary for ECCV submission?
items.summary: First-time ECCV submitter asks whether including keywords in a submission is required.

items.title: [D] Working on a photo-based calorie tracker app
items.summary: Developer building a photo-based calorie tracking app asks for ML approaches (detection+regression vs segmentation), datasets for portion-aware recognition, and feasibility of monocular depth on mobile.

items.title: Cursor Automations
items.summary: Post pointing to Cursor blog on Automations (Cursor product feature announcement).

items.title: GPT 5.4 is now available in Cursor and leading benchmarks
items.summary: Announcement that GPT-5.4 is available in Cursor and is performing strongly on benchmarks (Cursor community post).

items.title: Why are the GPT-5.4 models all in MAX mode by default?
items.summary: User questions Cursor's choice to set GPT-5.4 models to MAX mode by default despite same context window as 5.3 and claimed token-efficiency.

items.title: WARNING: Cursor Support’s official response to my $544 "rogue loop" charge proves their billing system is dangerously flawed.
items.summary: User reports a $544 charge caused by an automated agent loop hitting Cursor's backend at machine speed; claims backend bypassed UI safeguards and lacks server-side anomaly detection / kill-switches; warns others to hard-cap on-demand limits.

items.title: The importance of programming skill in AI-assisted development
items.summary: Discussion arguing that programmer fundamentals (system design, coding paradigms) remain crucial in AI-assisted development; model and agent config matter less than skillful prompting and codebase patterns.

items.title: Looking for Clarity on Cursors Rules vs Cursor Skills vs Claude Skills
items.summary: User asks how Cursor Rules, Cursor Skills, and Claude Code Skills differ and how to replicate modular 'load-when-relevant' skill behavior inside Cursor.

items.title: Discussion: Bringing Multi-Agent Debates to Cursor via MCP (AgentChatBus)
items.summary: Proposal and experiment (AgentChatBus) that integrates a persistent multi-agent message bus into Cursor to run parallel role-based debates (security, perf, etc.) and synthesize consensus for safer code edits; includes a gist with a conversation log.

items.title: Infinite refresh loop in cursor.com/agents ?
items.summary: User reports infinite refresh loop on cursor.com/agents preventing new agents; cleared cache/cookies and tried logout with no fix.

items.title: The memory leak is back! Single line edits to a file show the entire file rewritten and massive RAM demand. Scrolling past a file in chat bogs down the system.
items.summary: User reports a resurfaced memory leak in Cursor causing full-file rewrites for single-line edits and large RAM spikes when scrolling files in chat, severely slowing the app.

items.title: Working with Worktrees in Cursor
items.summary: User asks about workflows for using git worktrees in Cursor and whether Cursor merges worktree changes back into the original branch or supports independent workstreams.

items.title: I tried Claude Code, but I'm sticking with Cursor. Claude just acts like a "know-it-all"
items.summary: User compares Claude Code and Cursor: prefers Cursor's focused workflow; finds Claude tends to over-refactor and make architectural changes unsolicited.

items.title: How do you guys automate/bypass Phantom wallet approvals
items.summary: Developer building a Solana dApp asks for ways to speed frontend transaction testing (avoiding manual Phantom wallet approvals) during rapid AI-assisted dev—options considered: dev-mode burner keys, automated browser tests.

items.title: These 5 lines just reduced my MCP token usage from 50k to 4k
items.summary: User shares a small code/config tweak that reduced MCP token usage dramatically (from ~50k to ~4k tokens).

items.title: You can use this Control Tower for your Cursor Cloud Agents 🗼🛫
items.summary: Open-source terminal dashboard 'agents-control-tower' to manage Cursor cloud agents (launch, stop, follow-ups, delete) via keyboard; repo: github.com/ofershap/agents-control-tower.

items.title: Cursor visual issues
items.summary: User reports visual/UI issues after the latest Cursor update; tried reinstalling and opening dev tools; Cursor support engaged.

items.title: why model degradations happen?
items.summary: Short post asking why model performance degradations happen (community discussion).

items.title: I Haven't Written a Line of Code in Six Months
items.summary: Long-form reddit post by a veteran developer describing a shift from coding to managing Claude Code agents full-time; recounts productivity gains and identity shift.

items.title: Did you know Kojima named the Anthropic CEO?
items.summary: Lighthearted/viral reddit post referencing Kojima and Anthropic CEO (community meme/content).

items.title: Pentagon Formally Labels Anthropic Supply-Chain Risk, Escalating Conflict
items.summary: Link to WSJ reporting that the Pentagon formally labeled Anthropic as a supply-chain risk, signaling a security/policy escalation.

items.title: Going from Pro to Max 20x .. HOLYY
items.summary: User reports upgrading to Claude Max 20x greatly increased usage cap/capacity and improved experience despite cost.

items.title: Chatgpt 5.4 vs claude opus 4.6
items.summary: Community post comparing ChatGPT 5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 (user benchmark / impressions).

items.title: PRIVACY: Just a reminder to turn off "Help improve Claude" if you're concerned about your chats becoming part of Claude's training data
items.summary: Reminder screenshot/post advising users to disable 'Help improve Claude' if they don't want chats used for training.

items.title: The best thing about Claude is it doesn't want you to stay with it forever
items.summary: User praises Claude for concise thread endings and for helping users move on rather than prolonging interactions, contrasting with GPT's tendency to upsell follow-ups.

items.title: My wife kept nagging me so I built a harness to code for me instead. Won a hackathon with it.
items.summary: User built an automated Claude Code harness (open-source, MIT) to manage sessions and generate code via Socratic interview + parallel Claude sessions; used at hackathon, produced large output.

items.title: Is Claude salty recently ?
items.summary: User wonders whether Claude model responses have become 'salty' or different recently while using Opus 4.6.

items.title: 7.5m Tokens might be limit for Max 20x? Just hit 100% for the week.
items.summary: User reports hitting weekly token limit (7.5M tokens) on Claude Max 20x plan.

items.title: Claude desktop app silently downloads a 13 GB file on every launch — and you can't stop it
items.summary: User reports Claude desktop app auto-downloads a ~13GB 'claudevm.bundle' VM for Claude Code on every launch with no opt-out for individual users; support acknowledges behavior and no workaround available.

items.title: Claude feels strangely… calm compared to other AIs
items.summary: User observes Claude's tone is calmer and more uncertainty-aware compared to other models; asks whether this is model behavior or prompting difference.

items.title: 60 million Copilot code reviews and counting
items.summary: GitHub blog post describing Copilot Code Review usage and how it helps teams keep up with AI-accelerated code changes (60M reviews milestone).

items.title: Scaling AI opportunity across the globe: Learnings from GitHub and Andela
items.summary: GitHub blog post sharing learnings from Andela on how developers learn AI tools inside production workflows and scaling AI opportunity globally.

items.title: GPT-5.4 Thinking System Card
Introducing GPT-5.4
items.summary: Introducing GPT-5.4, OpenAI’s most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work, with state-of-the-art coding, computer use, tool search, and 1M-token context.

items.title: Reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought, and that’s good
items.summary: OpenAI introduces CoT-Control and reports that reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought, highlighting monitorability as an AI safety safeguard.

items.title: Ensuring AI use in education leads to opportunity
items.summary: OpenAI shares tools, certifications, and measurement resources to help schools and universities close AI capability gaps and expand opportunity.

items.title: The five AI value models driving business reinvention
items.summary: OpenAI outlines five AI value models leaders can use to sequence AI adoption from workforce fluency to process reinvention for durable business advantage.

items.title: Introducing ChatGPT for Excel and new financial data integrations
items.summary: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for Excel and new financial integrations powered by GPT-5.4 aimed at accelerating modeling and analysis in regulated environments.

items.title: Introducing the Adoption news channel
items.summary: OpenAI launches a new Adoption news channel focused on practical insights and frameworks to translate AI progress into business advantage.
